targets.name: rtx-pro-6000-workstation
targets.notes: ubuntu-server LAN host (RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell, 96GB). gpu_type intentionally lies as nvidia-tesla-t4 so the GCS scheduler yields T4 jobs to it; vram_gb drives the compatibility-list broadcast that also exposes nvidia-l4/nvidia-tesla-a100 slots since 96GB > 40GB > 24GB > 16GB tiers. Hostname `ubuntu-server` is what the agent matches against.

targets.name: ubuntu-server
targets.notes: Alias matching the actual hostname `ubuntu-server` so wc agent --auto's lookup_self() resolves on the box itself; same target as rtx-pro-6000-workstation.

targets.name: gcp-spot-t4
targets.notes: GCP T4 Spot dispatcher. Drives instance creation via wisent-compute cloud function.

coordinators.name: gcp-cloud-function
coordinators.notes: Default. wisent-compute-tick Cloud Function in us-central1, fired by Cloud Scheduler every 3 minutes. Deployed via deploy/redeploy_function.sh from CI.

coordinators.name: local-mac
coordinators.notes: Run `wc coordinator --target local-mac` on this Mac to drive the tick locally instead of GCP. State stays in GCS so cloud agents and local consumers all see the same queue. Set active=true to mark this as the primary; only one coordinator should be active at a time.
